title: Stores API
description: Stores represent the physical or virtual location of sales. Stores have to be created for each merchant before they can register terminals to accept payments. Onboarded merchants can have one or more stores created under them based on their business requirements.

apis.heading: Create Store
apis.description: Creates a store under a merchant. Use this API to create a store to which you can register your terminals. You can create a single store or multiple stores under a merchant based on your requirements.

reqParams.name: storeName
reqParams.description: Name of the store.

reqParams.name: email
reqParams.description: Email address of the store. Email address in mandatory to support online payments.

reqParams.name: phoneNumber
reqParams.description: Contact number of the store in code and number format.

reqParams.name: code
reqParams.description: International dialing code identifying the country or region.

reqParams.name: number
reqParams.description: A string of numbers ranging from 0-9 with a length of 5-15 characters.

reqParams.name: address
reqParams.description: Address of the store.

reqParams.name: addressLine1
reqParams.description: First line of address.

reqParams.name: addressLine2
reqParams.description: Second line of address.

reqParams.name: addressLine3
reqParams.description: Third line of address.

reqParams.name: city
reqParams.description: Name of the city where the store is located.

reqParams.name: countryCode
reqParams.description: Two-letter ISO country code in uppercase, representing the location of the store.

reqParams.name: postalCode
reqParams.description: Postal code of the store.

reqParams.name: acquirerMID
reqParams.description: Acquirer Merchant ID is a mandatory parameter for PF partners who want to support a store based acquiring model.

reqParams.name: onlineInfo
reqParams.description: In order to start accepting online payments you need to create an online store which requires some additional properties such as the following.

reqParams.name: merchantWebshopURL
reqParams.description: This is the web-shop URL of the merchant.

reqParams.name: paymentPageHostURL
reqParams.description: This is the URL of the payment page. This is required for integrating online payments using SDK mode.

reqParams.name: termsAndConditionsURL
reqParams.description: This is the URL of the T&C of the merchant’s web-shop, it has to contain the refund policy.

reqParams.name: privacyPolicyURL
reqParams.description: This is the URL of the privacy policy of the merchant.

resParams.name: status
resParams.description: Status of the request.

resParams.name: data
resParams.description: Response data.

resParams.name: storeId
resParams.description: Store ID of the created store.

resParams.name: merchantId
resParams.description: The Merchant ID of the merchant under which the store is created.

resParams.name: name
resParams.description: Name of the store.

resParams.name: address
resParams.description: Address of the store.

resParams.name: careOf
resParams.description: Name of the addressee that is accepting the correspondence for the intended recipient.

resParams.name: addressLine1
resParams.description: First line of address.

resParams.name: addressLine2
resParams.description: Second line of address.

resParams.name: addressLine3
resParams.description: Third line of address.

resParams.name: city
resParams.description: Name of the City where the store is located.

resParams.name: countryCode
resParams.description: Two-letter ISO country code in uppercase, representing the location of the store.

resParams.name: postalCode
resParams.description: Postal Code of the place where the store is located.

resParams.name: phone
resParams.description: Contact number of the store.

resParams.name: email
resParams.description: Email address of the store.

resParams.name: merchantURLDomainVerficationKey
resParams.description: This is a key to set and verify that the merchant owns the domain of the URL.

resParams.name: paymentPageURLDomainVerficationKey
resParams.description: This is a key to set and verify the payment page domain URL.

resParams.name: message
resParams.description: A message that describes the status of the request

apis.heading: Deactivate a Store
apis.description: Deactivates a store. Use this API to deactivate any store under a merchant. You can delete a store instantly or schedule it to be deleted at a later date. Stores can be deleted only if there are no terminals registered to it. If a store has terminals registered to it, you will have to delink the terminal from the store or change the terminal to another store under the same merchant.

reqParams.name: deactivationDate
reqParams.description: Date of deactivation of the store in yyyy-mm-dd format.

resParams.name: status
resParams.description: Status of the request.

resParams.name: message
resParams.description: Message describing the status of store deactivation.

apis.heading: Fetch Store Details
apis.description: Retrieves information for a specific store. Use this API to get information regarding a particular store created under a merchant.

resParams.name: status
resParams.description: Status of the request.

resParams.name: data
resParams.description: Response data.

resParams.name: storeId
resParams.description: Store ID of the store.

resParams.name: merchantId
resParams.description: The Merchant ID of the merchant under which the store is created.

resParams.name: name
resParams.description: Name of the store.

resParams.name: status
resParams.description: Status of the store.

possibleValues.description: The store is active and ready for payments.
The store is deactivated.
The store is blocked from further activities.
The store is in verification stage.

resParams.name: onlineOnboardingStatus
resParams.description: Onboarding status of the online store.

possibleValues.description: The store is approved for online payments.
The online store verification is initiated.
Failed to verify the online store.

resParams.name: address
resParams.description: Address of the store.

resParams.name: careOf
resParams.description: Name of the addressee that is accepting the correspondence for the intended recipient.

resParams.name: addressLine1
resParams.description: The first line of the store's address.

resParams.name: addressLine2
resParams.description: The second line of the store's address.

resParams.name: addressLine3
resParams.description: The third line of the store's address.

resParams.name: city
resParams.description: Name of the City where the store is located.

resParams.name: countryCode
resParams.description: Two-letter ISO country code in uppercase, representing the location of the store.

resParams.name: postalCode
resParams.description: Postal Code of the place where the store is located.

resParams.name: phone
resParams.description: Contact number of the store.

resParams.name: email
resParams.description: Email address of the store.

resParams.name: onlineInfo
resParams.description: In order to start accepting online payments you need to create an online store which requires some additional properties such as the following.

resParams.name: merchantWebshopURL
resParams.description: This is the web-shop URL of the merchant.

resParams.name: paymentPageHostURL
resParams.description: This is the URL of the payment page.

resParams.name: termsAndConditionsURL
resParams.description: This is the URL of the T&C of the merchant’s web-shop, it has to contain the refund policy.

resParams.name: privacyPolicyURL
resParams.description: This is the URL of the privacy policy of the merchant.

resParams.name: message
resParams.description: Message describing the status of the request.

response.response:
{
	"status": "SUCCESS",
	"data": {
		"storeId": "824bd4a739dc200b0f",
		"merchantId": "81a641f8b3cfd0070e",

apis.heading: Update Store Details
apis.description: Updates the store details. You can use this API to edit the name, address information,  email address, and phone number of a store.

reqParams.name: storeName
reqParams.description: Name of the store.

reqParams.name: email
reqParams.description: Email address of the store.

reqParams.name: phoneNumber
reqParams.description: Contact number of the store in code and number format.

reqParams.name: code
reqParams.description: International dialing code identifying the country or region.

reqParams.name: number
reqParams.description: A string of numbers ranging from 0-9 with a length of 5-15 characters

reqParams.name: address
reqParams.description: Address of the store.

reqParams.name: addressLine1
reqParams.description: First line of address.

reqParams.name: addressLine2
reqParams.description: Second line of address.

reqParams.name: addressLine3
reqParams.description: Third line of address.

reqParams.name: city
reqParams.description: City where the store is located.

reqParams.name: countryCode
reqParams.description: Two-letter ISO country code in uppercase, representing the location of the store.

reqParams.name: postalCode
reqParams.description: Postal code of the store.

reqParams.name: onlineInfo
reqParams.description: In order to update physical store into online store, some additional properties are required. Online Info can be updated only once.

reqParams.name: merchantWebshopURL
reqParams.description: This is the web-shop URL of the merchant.

reqParams.name: paymentPageHostURL
reqParams.description: This is the URL of the payment page. Required for integrating online payments using SDK mode.

reqParams.name: termsAndConditionsURL
reqParams.description: This is the URL of the T&C of the merchant’s web-shop, it has to contain the refund policy.

reqParams.name: privacyPolicyURL
reqParams.description: This is the URL of the privacy policy of the merchant.

resParams.name: status
resParams.description: Status of the request. 'SUCCESS' | 'ERROR'.

resParams.name: data
resParams.description: Response data.

resParams.name: merchantURLDomainVerficationKey
resParams.description: This is a key to set and verify that the merchant owns the domain of the URL.

resParams.name: paymentPageURLDomainVerficationKey
resParams.description: This is a key to set and verify the payment page domain URL.

resParams.name: message
resParams.description: Message describing the status of the request.

apis.heading: Fetch All Store Terminals
apis.query: To query terminals by terminal type, send in terminalType as query param.
apis.description: Retrieves a list of all terminals associated with a specific store. You can use this API to get information regarding all the terminals registered under a store.

reqParams.name: terminalType
reqParams.description: Optional query param to filter terminals by terminal type.

possibleValues.description: Hardware terminal with a physical keypad.
Android terminal with custom payment apps, digital receipts etc.
Android terminal with a built-in printer.
Complete checkout solution with a customer facing screen.
Software checkout solution for NFC-enabled Android devices to accept contactless card payments.
Surfboard-hosted payment page where all UI and configurations are managed by Surfboard.
Merchant-hosted payment page where Surfboard renders payment fields within the merchant’s environment.
Used for Merchant Initiated Transactions (MIT), allowing payments to be triggered by the merchant without customer interaction.
Standalone printing device.
Surfboard Android based test terminal used during integrations.

resParams.name: status
resParams.description: Status of the request. 'SUCCESS' | 'ERROR'.

resParams.name: data
resParams.description: Response data.

resParams.name: terminalId
resParams.description: Store ID of the terminal.

resParams.name: terminalType
resParams.description: Describes the type of the terminal.

possibleValues.description: Hardware terminal with a physical keypad.
Android terminal with custom payment apps, digital receipts etc.
Android terminal with a built-in printer.
Complete checkout solution with a customer facing screen.
Software checkout solution for NFC-enabled Android devices to accept contactless card payments.
Surfboard-hosted payment page where all UI and configurations are managed by Surfboard.
Merchant-hosted payment page where Surfboard renders payment fields within the merchant’s environment.
Used for Merchant Initiated Transactions (MIT), allowing payments to be triggered by the merchant without customer interaction.
Standalone printing device.
Surfboard Android based test terminal used during integrations.

resParams.name: softwareVersion
resParams.description: Software version of the terminal.

resParams.name: osType
resParams.description: Describes the type of operating system running on the terminals.

possibleValues.description: Android operating system.
Apple's operating system for iPhones.
Surfboard firmware.

resParams.name: osVersion
resParams.description: Operating System(OS) version of the terminal.

resParams.name: deviceVendor
resParams.description: Device vendor of the terminal.

resParams.name: deviceModel
resParams.description: Device model of the terminal.

resParams.name: serialNo
resParams.description: Serial number of the terminal.

resParams.name: storeId
resParams.description: The Store ID of the store under which the terminal is registered.

resParams.name: linkedTerminalId
resParams.description: The Terminal ID of another terminal to which the current terminal is linked.

resParams.name: terminalStatus
resParams.description: Status of the terminal. The terminal can be in any one of these states: 'REGISTERED' | 'ACTIVE' | 'IN_ACTIVE' | 'DE_REGISTERED’.

resParams.name: terminalPaymentMethods
resParams.description: Payment methods supported by the terminal.

resParams.name: startDate
resParams.description: Timestamp at which terminal was registered.

resParams.name: terminalName
resParams.description: Name of the terminal used as a reference for users.

resParams.name: message
resParams.description: Message describing the status of the request.

apis.heading: Verify Store Domain
apis.description: Use this API to trigger a verify domain request with Surfboard.

reqParams.name: domainType
reqParams.description: Specify the domain to be verified.

possibleValues.description: Use this to verify the merchant webshop domain.
To verify the payment page host domain use this.

resParams.name: status
resParams.description: Status of the request.

resParams.name: message
resParams.description: Message describing the status of domain verification of store.

apis.heading: Fetch Store Domains
apis.description: This API retrieves the details of the store's domain.

resParams.name: status
resParams.description: Status of the request.

resParams.name: data
resParams.description: Response data.

resParams.name: onlineStoreStatus
resParams.description: Status of the online store.

possibleValues.description: The online store is approved for online payments.
The online store verification is initiated.
Failed to verify the online store.

resParams.name: domains
resParams.description: Contains the domain details.

resParams.name: status
resParams.description: The current status of the domain.

possibleValues.description: The domain is active.
The domain is deactivated.

resParams.name: type
resParams.description: Refers to the type of the domain.

possibleValues.description: This represents the merchant webshop domain.
This represents the payment page host.

resParams.name: name
resParams.description: Name of the domain.

resParams.name: verificationKey
resParams.description: This is the key set in your domain which is used for verification.

resParams.name: isDomainVerified
resParams.description: Indicates whether the domain has been verified.

resParams.name: message
resParams.description: Message describing the status of domain verification of store.
